No Survival. None of the representations, warranties, covenants, or agreements in this Agreement or in any instrument delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall survive the Closing, and all rights, claims, and causes of action (whether in contract, tort, or otherwise, or whether at law or in equity) with respect thereto shall terminate at the Closing. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither this Section 6.01 nor anything else in this Agreement to the contrary shall limit: (a) the survival of any covenant or agreement of the parties that, by its terms, is required to be performed or complied with in whole or in part after the Closing, which covenants and agreements shall survive the Closing in accordance with their respective terms; or (b) the liability of any Person with respect to fraud. Governing Law; Arbitration. All questions concerning the construction, validity, enforcement, and interpretation of this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York without giving effect to any choice or conflict of law provision or rule thereof.

Each party irrevocably waives, to the fullest extent it may effectively do so, any objection which it may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any such arbitration in Hong Kong and the HKIAC, and hereby submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of HKIAC in any such arbitration. The award of the arbitration tribunal shall be conclusive and binding upon the disputing parties, and any party to the dispute may apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for enforcement of such award. Any party to the dispute shall be entitled to seek preliminary injunctive relief, if possible, from any court of competent jurisdiction pending the constitution of the arbitral tribunal. Section 6.03  
Successors and Assigns. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors and assigns. Neither this Agreement nor any of the rights, interests, or obligations hereunder shall be assigned by any of the parties hereto (whether by operation of law or otherwise) without the prior written consent of the other parties;

In addition, each Party may disclose, after giving prior notice to the other Parties to the extent practicable under the circumstances and subject to any practicable arrangements to protect confidentiality, Confidential Information to the extent required under judicial or regulatory process or in connection with any judicial process regarding any legal action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or any Transaction Document; provided that, the Party who is required to make such disclosure shall, to the extent permitted by law and so far as it is practicable, at the other Parties’ request and at the requesting Party’s cost, cooperate with the other Parties to enable such other Parties to seek an appropriate protection order or remedy. summary: **Comprehensive Summary of LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond due 2029 Terms and Conditions**

The LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond, maturing on [●], 2029, is structured to provide bondholders with a principal amount due at maturity and an annual interest rate of 6.0%. Interest payments will be made in arrears, commencing on [●], 2025, to those bondholders recorded as of one Clearing System Business Day prior to the Interest Payment Date. The interest calculation is based on a 360-day year.

This bond was issued under a Subscription Agreement dated [●], 2024, between Lotus Technology Inc. (the Issuer) and Kershaw Health Limited (the Bondholder). The bond's governance is further defined by a Fiscal Agency Agreement dated [●], 2024, with Deutsche Bank AG serving as the fiscal agent.

The Issuer retains the right to repurchase the bonds for cancellation prior to the Repurchase Date, which is defined as 30 days post-issue or another mutually agreed date. Such repurchase must be communicated to bondholders at least 7 Business Days before the Repurchase Date, and will occur at the principal amount.

After the full payment of the principal, the Issuer may opt for optional redemption of the bond at 100% of the principal plus any accrued interest, provided that notice is given 30 to 60 days in advance. It is important to note that bonds with a principal amount of $200,000 or less cannot be partially redeemed.

The bond is issued in registered form, with minimum denominations set at $200,000. In circumstances where the common depositary is unavailable, the Issuer will issue Definitive Certificates in exchange for the Global Certificate.

Events of Default are clearly defined and include: (a) a 30-day default in interest payments; (b) default in principal payments; (c) defaults by the Issuer or its Significant Subsidiaries on debts exceeding $100 million; (d) failure to pay judgments over $100 million; (e) initiation of voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy proceedings; and (f) involuntary proceedings that remain undismissed for 60 days.

This summary provides a comprehensive overview of the key terms, conditions, and obligations associated with the LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond due 2029, ensuring clarity on financial metrics, legal stipulations, and procedural requirements for potential investors and stakeholders.

summary: **Comprehensive Summary of ARTICLE VI - MISCELLANEOUS**

**Section 6.01 - No Survival:** Upon Closing, all representations, warranties, covenants, or agreements outlined in this Agreement will terminate, with the exception of those obligations that are to be performed post-Closing and any liabilities arising from fraudulent actions.

**Section 6.02 - Governing Law; Arbitration:** This Agreement is governed by the laws of New York. Any disputes arising from the Agreement will be resolved through arbitration in Hong Kong, following the rules set by the Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (HKIAC). The arbitration will involve three arbitrators: each party will appoint one, and the third will be selected by the Chairman of HKIAC. All proceedings will be conducted in English, and both parties waive any objections to the venue being in Hong Kong.

**Section 6.03 - Successors and Assigns:** The Agreement is binding on the parties and their respective successors. Assignments of rights under the Agreement require prior written consent, although the Subscriber is permitted to assign rights to Affiliates with prior notice.

**Section 6.04 - Further Assurances:** The parties are obligated to cooperate in executing necessary documentation and obtaining any required approvals to facilitate the transactions contemplated by the Agreement.

**Section 6.05 - Notices:** All communications regarding the Agreement must be in writing and delivered either personally, via courier, or through email during business hours. Such communications will be considered effective upon receipt.

**Section 6.06 - Severability:** Should any provision of the Agreement be deemed invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue to be valid and enforceable.

**Section 6.07 - Entire Agreement; Amendments:** This Agreement constitutes the complete understanding between the parties, superseding any prior agreements. Amendments to the Agreement can only be made in writing and must be signed by both parties.

**Section 6.08 - Confidentiality:** Both parties are required to maintain the confidentiality of any non-public information. Disclosure is only permitted when legally mandated or to Affiliates on a need-to-know basis. The obligation to maintain confidentiality survives the termination of the Agreement.

**Section 6.09 - Specific Performance:** The parties recognize that breaches of the Agreement may lead to irreparable harm, thus allowing for the possibility of seeking injunctions to enforce the terms of the Agreement.

**Section 6.10 - Termination:** The Agreement may be terminated prior to Closing by mutual consent, due to legal prohibitions, or if any representations or warranties become untrue or are breached without remedy within seven Business Days. Upon termination, the Agreement becomes void, except for the provisions contained in ARTICLE VI.

**Section 6.11 - Execution in Counterparts:** The Agreement may be executed in counterparts, which may include electronically imaged signatures.

**Section 6.12 - Remedies and Waivers:** Delays in the exercise of any rights under the Agreement do not constitute a waiver of those rights. The rights provided within the Agreement are cumulative and not exclusive.

This Agreement is executed by authorized representatives from Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our business 
summary: The company is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the prestigious British brand "Lotus," which has a rich history of over 70 years in racing and automotive excellence. Acquired by Geely Holding in 2017, Lotus is transitioning from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, supported by Geely's asset-light model and extensive R&D capabilities. The luxury BEV market is projected to grow significantly, and Lotus aims to capitalize on this opportunity by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, which debuted in 2022. The Eletre features advanced technology, impressive performance metrics, and a competitive price point, contributing to a strong global order book of over 8,600 units as of September 30, 2023.

In addition to the Eletre, the company plans to introduce the Emeya lifestyle vehicle and two more fully electric models by 2025. Its proprietary 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture enhances vehicle performance and production efficiency, while a cutting-edge Driver Assistance System incorporates advanced safety features. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely in China, leveraging their facilities and supply chain to ensure scalability and quality.

The company employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales strategy, enhancing customer engagement through personalized services and a global distribution network. With 206 stores worldwide and plans to expand to over 300 by 2025, Lotus is well-positioned to enhance its brand presence and meet the growing demand for luxury BEVs, supported by a cumulative order book exceeding 19,000 units as of late September 2023.

title: Our Business 
summary: We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer under the esteemed British brand "Lotus," which has over 70 years of racing heritage and is renowned for its performance, design, and engineering excellence. With the support of Geely Holding, which acquired a 51% stake in Lotus in 2017, we are transitioning from traditional sports cars to high-performance electric vehicles, leveraging cutting-edge technology and a robust research and development framework. 

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow significantly, is currently underserved, with only a handful of luxury BEV models available compared to a multitude of internal combustion engine options. As a leader in this sector, we aim to launch our E-segment BEV model and achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. Our first fully electric model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, debuted in 2022, showcasing advanced technology and exceptional performance, with plans for further electric vehicle introductions in the coming years.

Our proprietary 800-volt Electric Power Architecture enables rapid model introductions and efficient production, while our Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) incorporates cutting-edge features like deployable LiDAR. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at their advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, enhancing scalability and supply chain management.

We prioritize a luxury retail experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores and a global digital platform that enables vehicle customization and direct customer engagement. With a growing global distribution network and plans to expand our store presence, we delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, including both lifestyle and sports cars, and are poised for continued growth in the luxury BEV market.

title: Bond Subscription Agreement 
summary: On November 7, 2024, the Company entered into a Bond Subscription Agreement with Kershaw Health Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England and Wales. Under this agreement, Kershaw Health Limited will subscribe for and purchase a senior bond with a principal amount of $500,000,000, which is due in 2029. The subscription price for the bond is set at 100% of the principal amount, amounting to $500,000,000. The Subscriber is obligated to pay this subscription price to the Company within 30 days following the bond's issue date.

Additionally, the Company retains the right to repurchase the Bond, in whole but not in part, at a price equal to the aggregate principal amount paid by the Subscriber up to the repurchase date, provided that the purchase price has not been fully paid. The Bond Subscription Agreement, along with the Form of Global Certificate, is included in the current report on Form 6-K as Exhibits 10.1 and 10.2, respectively. The description of the agreement is qualified by reference to these documents, ensuring compliance with relevant regulatory and legal requirements.

**Section 6.01 - No Survival:** Upon Closing, all representations, warranties, covenants, or agreements outlined in this Agreement will terminate, with the exception of those obligations that are to be performed post-Closing and any liabilities arising from fraudulent actions.

Any disputes arising from the Agreement will be resolved through arbitration in Hong Kong, following the rules set by the Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (HKIAC). The arbitration will involve three arbitrators: each party will appoint one, and the third will be selected by the Chairman of HKIAC. All proceedings will be conducted in English, and both parties waive any objections to the venue being in Hong Kong.

**Section 6.03 - Successors and Assigns:** The Agreement is binding on the parties and their respective successors. Assignments of rights under the Agreement require prior written consent, although the Subscriber is permitted to assign rights to Affiliates with prior notice.

**Section 6.04 - Further Assurances:** The parties are obligated to cooperate in executing necessary documentation and obtaining any required approvals to facilitate the transactions contemplated by the Agreement.

**Section 6.05 - Notices:** All communications regarding the Agreement must be in writing and delivered either personally, via courier, or through email during business hours. Such communications will be considered effective upon receipt.

**Section 6.06 - Severability:** Should any provision of the Agreement be deemed invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue to be valid and enforceable.

**Section 6.07 - Entire Agreement; Amendments:** This Agreement constitutes the complete understanding between the parties, superseding any prior agreements. Amendments to the Agreement can only be made in writing and must be signed by both parties.

**Section 6.08 - Confidentiality:** Both parties are required to maintain the confidentiality of any non-public information. Disclosure is only permitted when legally mandated or to Affiliates on a need-to-know basis. The obligation to maintain confidentiality survives the termination of the Agreement.

**Section 6.09 - Specific Performance:** The parties recognize that breaches of the Agreement may lead to irreparable harm, thus allowing for the possibility of seeking injunctions to enforce the terms of the Agreement.

**Section 6.10 - Termination:** The Agreement may be terminated prior to Closing by mutual consent, due to legal prohibitions, or if any representations or warranties become untrue or are breached without remedy within seven Business Days. Upon termination, the Agreement becomes void, except for the provisions contained in ARTICLE VI.

**Section 6.11 - Execution in Counterparts:** The Agreement may be executed in counterparts, which may include electronically imaged signatures.

**Section 6.12 - Remedies and Waivers:** Delays in the exercise of any rights under the Agreement do not constitute a waiver of those rights. The rights provided within the Agreement are cumulative and not exclusive.

This Agreement is executed by authorized representatives from Lotus Technology Inc. and Kershaw Health Limited.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement, or the interpretation, breach, termination, or validity hereof, shall be submitted to arbitration upon the request of any party with notice to the other party. The arbitration shall be conducted in Hong Kong under the auspices of the Hong Kong International Arbitration Centre (“HKIAC”) in accordance with the HKIAC Administered Arbitration Rules then in effect, which rules are deemed to be incorporated by reference into this Section 6.02. There shall be three (3) arbitrators. The complainant and the respondent to such dispute shall each select one arbitrator within thirty (30) days after giving or receiving the demand for arbitration. The Chairman of the HKIAC shall select the third arbitrator, who shall be qualified to practice law in Hong Kong. If either party to the arbitration does not appoint an arbitrator who has consented to participate within thirty (30) days after selection of the first arbitrator, the relevant appointment shall be made by the Chairman of the HKIAC. The arbitration proceedings shall be conducted in English.

summary: **Comprehensive Summary of LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond due 2029 Terms and Conditions**

The LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond, maturing on [●], 2029, is structured to provide bondholders with a principal amount due at maturity and an annual interest rate of 6.0%. Interest payments will be made in arrears, commencing on [●], 2025, to those bondholders recorded as of one Clearing System Business Day prior to the Interest Payment Date. The interest calculation is based on a 360-day year.

This bond was issued under a Subscription Agreement dated [●], 2024, between Lotus Technology Inc. (the Issuer) and Kershaw Health Limited (the Bondholder). The bond's governance is further defined by a Fiscal Agency Agreement dated [●], 2024, with Deutsche Bank AG serving as the fiscal agent.

The Issuer retains the right to repurchase the bonds for cancellation prior to the Repurchase Date, which is defined as 30 days post-issue or another mutually agreed date. Such repurchase must be communicated to bondholders at least 7 Business Days before the Repurchase Date, and will occur at the principal amount.

After the full payment of the principal, the Issuer may opt for optional redemption of the bond at 100% of the principal plus any accrued interest, provided that notice is given 30 to 60 days in advance. It is important to note that bonds with a principal amount of $200,000 or less cannot be partially redeemed.

The bond is issued in registered form, with minimum denominations set at $200,000. In circumstances where the common depositary is unavailable, the Issuer will issue Definitive Certificates in exchange for the Global Certificate.

Events of Default are clearly defined and include: (a) a 30-day default in interest payments; (b) default in principal payments; (c) defaults by the Issuer or its Significant Subsidiaries on debts exceeding $100 million; (d) failure to pay judgments over $100 million; (e) initiation of voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy proceedings; and (f) involuntary proceedings that remain undismissed for 60 days.

This summary provides a comprehensive overview of the key terms, conditions, and obligations associated with the LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. 6.0% Bond due 2029, ensuring clarity on financial metrics, legal stipulations, and procedural requirements for potential investors and stakeholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: (c)  History of the Group and basis of presentation 
summary: The Lotus BEV business was established in 2018 by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group ("Geely Holding") through its subsidiaries, including Wuhan Lotus Cars Co., Ltd., the Lotus BEV unit of Ningbo Geely R&D in China, Lotus Tech Creative Centre Limited in the UK, and Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH in Germany, all under the control of Mr. Li Shufu.

On August 9, 2021, the Company was incorporated as a limited liability entity in the Cayman Islands, with Lotus Advanced Technology Limited Partnership ("Founders Offshore Vehicle") subscribing for 191,981,772 ordinary shares. The Four Core Investors, including Ming Jun Holdings Limited (owned by Mr. Li Shufu), entered into an agreement to act in concert, which granted Mr. Li Shufu majority voting rights over the Founders Offshore Vehicle.

Subsequently, on December 29, 2020, Geely Holding and Ningbo Juhe Yinqing Enterprise Management Consulting Partnership established Wuhan Lotus Technology Limited Company ("WFOE"), with Geely Holding owning a 60% stake and Founders Onshore Vehicle holding 40%, both entities controlled by Mr. Li Shufu.

On September 24, 2021, Etika Automotive SDN BHD, through its subsidiary Lotus Advanced Technology Limited ("Lotus HK"), acquired a 33.33% equity interest in WFOE. This transaction resulted in the following equity distribution: Geely Holding with 22.22%, Etika with 33.33%, and Founders Onshore Vehicle with 44.45%.

On November 11, 2021, the Company issued 143,986,329 ordinary shares to Etika in exchange for a 100% equity interest in Lotus HK. Following this, on December 15, 2021, Lotus HK acquired all equity interests in WFOE held by Geely Holding and Founders Onshore Vehicle, consolidating ownership and control within the group.

This comprehensive summary encapsulates the establishment, incorporation, and ownership structure of the Lotus BEV business, highlighting key dates, ownership percentages, and the roles of various stakeholders in the company's development.

All applicable share and per share amounts in the consolidated and combined financial statements have been retrospectively adjusted to reflect the effects of the recapitalization (“Recapitalization”); (ii) All the preferred shares of Lotus Technology that were issued and outstanding immediately prior to the effective time of the First Merger were converted to Lotus Technology ordinary shares on a one-for-one basis (the “Preferred Share Conversion”) and recapitalized into 68,228,526 ordinary shares using the Recapitalization Factor. metadata.title_summary:
title: (b) Merger and recapitalization 
summary: On February 22, 2024, the Company successfully completed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), following a merger agreement that was initially established on January 31, 2023, and later amended on October 11, 2023. The merger process involved two main steps: first, the Company's wholly-owned subsidiary, Lotus Temp Limited, merged with LCAA, making LCAA a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company and converting its shareholders into shareholders of the Company. Subsequently, the surviving entity from this merger merged with another subsidiary, Lotus EV Limited, resulting in the latter becoming the surviving entity. This series of transactions is collectively known as the Merger Transaction or Business Combination, which marks a significant milestone for the Company, indicating the finalization of all necessary agreements and regulatory approvals.

In conjunction with the merger, the Company underwent a recapitalization, converting all ordinary shares held by existing shareholders into 474,621,603 ordinary shares at a recapitalization factor of 0.2215. Additionally, prior to the merger's effective time, all preferred shares were converted into ordinary shares on a one-for-one basis, resulting in 68,228,526 ordinary shares. These changes necessitated retrospective adjustments in the consolidated financial statements. The Company also issued various ordinary shares as part of several financial transactions, including 35,849,458 shares to PIPE Investors, 36,597,038 shares to WFOE Exchangeable Notes Holders, and others related to the conversion of notes and investments. Notably, 50,000,000 shares were issued to Meritz Securities Co., Ltd. for $500,000, with the proceeds being invested in treasury securities. Overall, the merger and recapitalization are expected to enhance the Company's market position and operational efficiencies, paving the way for future growth and strategic initiatives.

The Lotus Emira GT4 is the perfect starting point for a high-performance race car. Everyone at Lotus is excited to be returning to world motorsport and to be doing so with such a competitive race car. The Lotus Emira GT4 was officially launched in May of 2022 with a dynamic demonstration on the Hethel test track, the iconic 2.2-mile circuit that has hosted the development of some of the world's most famous race cars. At the exclusive event, VIP guests were treated to passenger laps of the circuit with Gavan Kershaw at the wheel.

The culmination of more than 70 years of innovation on the road and track, the Emira GT4 marked the start of an exciting new era in performance GT racing for Lotus. It was the first new model from Lotus Advanced Performance, the bespoke vehicle and experiential division of the business launched earlier in the year. The return to world motorsport is a key pillar of the company's transformation from a UK sports car company to a global performance car business and brand.

Lotus aimed to build a limited number of Type 131R Emira GT4 race cars for the 2022 season, with plans to increase production for 2023 in line with global demand. The car's stats are as follows: Model: Emira GT4, Launch Date: May 2022, Track: Hethel test track, Length: 2.2 miles, Driver: Gavan Kershaw.

Stiffer suspension set-up for enhanced dynamic capability and feel. Gavan Kershaw, Director of Vehicle Attributes at Lotus, commented, "The Emira has a wider track than any recent Lotus road car, enhancing stability, delivering exceptional road-holding and the sensation of a very low centre of gravity. It is a true Lotus sports car that builds on the legacy of the Elise, Exige, and Evora."

Be one of the firm's most ambitious yet. When we mentioned to the design team and some of our contractors that we were going to go to Le Mans, they didn't think it would have 2011 in the date; they were looking at 2012! But everything that Lotus is doing at the moment is aggressive timing, and we're delivering everything that we say we're going to do," Lotus Motorsport Product Manager Gavan Kershaw stated. At first glance, the new GTE-spec Evora racecar may have seemed to have a similar appearance to the Evora GT4, which debuted in 2010, but as Kershaw explained, "roughly 85 percent of the car is brand-new, with the production chassis remaining one of the only key components that's been carried over." With their Cosworth-tuned engines bored out from 3.5 to 4.0 litres and all-new bodywork as well as larger wheels and uprights, the Nicola Scimeca-designed Evora GTE was almost 120lb heavier than the GT4 model, yet was still considered a lightweight in the GTE racing category. Despite the engineering challenges, the program remained relatively on schedule, with the first Evora GTE hitting the track in early April and completing around 1,000 kilometers of testing.

Drivers Mr. Cui Yue and Mr. Gaoxiang Fan brought a Chinese racing pedigree to Lotus motorsport. Both were graduates of the Geely University and had gone through their motorsport apprenticeships with the company’s sponsorship. 25-year-old Gaoxiang had enjoyed success in the China Rally Championship and was the 2017 Touring Car Champion of China. Cui, 29, had clocked up wins including the 2019.

At the Shanghai Auto Show 2019, Lotus Cars announced that they would be making a return to the international racing arena with the launch of the Evora GT4 Concept. With a factory-backed team aiming to take part in a full 2020 season, successful Chinese racing drivers Cui Yue and Gaoxiang Fan were appointed as its drivers.

Evora GT4 Concept:
- Type: 124
- Test and development driver: Gavan Kershaw

Gavan Kershaw explained, “Every Lotus is famed for its performance in ride and handling on challenging roads, surfaces, and through tight corners. This performance is borne from years of motorsport experience and testing at our HQ test track in Hethel, UK. All of which delivers confidence, response, and feel to the driver.” The Lotus reputation as a leader in intelligent engineering was preserved through the car’s use of a lightweight carbon-composite.
